methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Deep Atomic is a pre-seed nuclear energy company founded by William G.J. Theron. The valuation universe consists of 11 nuclear energy peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. While 8 same-sector private peers exist, none satisfy the filtering criteria for stage alignment or valuation tier comparability required for a robust median multiple. Specifically, the dataset contains zero pre-seed or adjacent-stage peers and zero similar-valuation-tier matches. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial comparability error and is therefore rejected.

Given the absence of sufficient pre-seed nuclear energy comparables and the inapplicability of peer triangulation, a traditional DCF approach or founder-track-record method would be required to establish a defensible valuation range for Deep Atomic. The primary constraint on this analysis is the scarcity of pre-seed-stage nuclear companies with disclosed terms. Any valuation estimate must account for pre-seed-stage risk premiums and the capital intensity typical of nuclear development, which are not directly observable in available peer data. Further diligence on comparable seed-stage investments in adjacent deep-tech sectors or direct founder reference checks would be necessary to narrow valuation bounds.
